title: Phonetics and Phonology
description: Speech sound production and patterning for intelligibility, rhythm, contrast, and pronunciation-aware explanation.
upgradePremise: Expanded for Nyx and Marion with stronger grammatical reasoning, case-based analysis, modules, projects, and explicit source coverage.

forNyx: Prefer precise, grammatical phrasing that sounds natural rather than stiff.
Use register-sensitive wording so conversational output and formal output do not collapse into one style.
When uncertain, preserve meaning first, then improve form through revision rather than over-correcting.
forMarion: Use the pack to explain why a wording choice works, not just whether it is 'right' or 'wrong'.
Ground revision advice in form-function reasoning, register, audience, and evidence.
Support coaching, diagnostics, and portfolio-style improvement tracking.

topics.name: Phonetics Essentials
coreClaims: Articulatory features explain how consonants and vowels are produced (place/manner/voicing).
Transcription is a tool for precision and pattern discovery.
practice: Allophones: identify predictable variants in context.

topics.name: Phonology Patterns
coreClaims: Phonological rules describe systematic alternations (e.g., assimilation) without treating them as errors.
Stress and rhythm affect intelligibility and perceived fluency.
practice: Mark stress patterns in multisyllabic words.
Explain a reduction pattern in fast speech vs careful speech.

modules.name: Consonants and Vowels
outcomes: Use place, manner, and voicing to describe consonants.
Use height, backness, and rounding to describe vowels.

modules.name: Phonemic Contrast and Allophony
outcomes: Explain predictable sound variants without calling them errors.

modules.name: Stress, Rhythm, and Intelligibility
outcomes: Explain how stress and reduction affect perceived fluency and clarity.

caseStudies.title: Pronunciation Feedback Without Shaming
caseStudies.scenario: A learner needs help with intelligibility, but correction risks becoming overly prescriptive or accent-policing.
intervention: Separate accent difference from communication breakdown.
Use contrastive examples rather than generic correction.

projects.name: Intelligibility Priority Map
projects.goal: Identify which sound contrasts and stress patterns matter most for understandable spoken output in assistant scenarios.

referenceTextsUsed.title: Some Questions and Answers about Grammar
referenceTextsUsed.organization: National Council of Teachers of English

auditSummary.notes: Normalized for consistent Marion retrieval, alias routing, and source coverage tracking.

normalizationStatus.normalizedFor: Marion/Nyx domain registry and Compose ingestion
